description.en: A large, dense wheat sourdough from Portugal's Alentejo plains, baked deliberately without steam so the crust turns thick and matte rather than glossy. Mixed by hand in a wooden trough and slapped to strength, it's the loaf behind açorda and countless Alentejan bread soups.
description.de: Ein großes, dichtes Weizensauerteigbrot von Portugals Alentejo-Ebenen, bewusst ohne Dampf gebacken, sodass die Kruste dick und matt statt glänzend wird. Von Hand in einem Holztrog gemischt und zur Stärke geschlagen, das Brot hinter açorda und unzähligen Brotsuppen des Alentejo.
description.es: Un pan de masa madre de trigo, grande y denso, de las llanuras del Alentejo portugués, horneado deliberadamente sin vapor para que la corteza quede gruesa y mate en vez de brillante. Amasado a mano en una artesa de madera, es el pan detrás de la açorda y las sopas del Alentejo.
description.fr: Un grand pain au levain de blé, dense, des plaines de l'Alentejo portugais, cuit volontairement sans vapeur pour que la croûte reste épaisse et mate plutôt que brillante. Pétri à la main dans un pétrin en bois, c'est le pain derrière l'açorda et les soupes de pain de l'Alentejo.

origin.note: Traditionally mixed and kneaded by hand in a wooden trough (masseira); baked with no steam, which is why the crust stays thick and matte rather than glossy.

yield.shape: large round hearth loaf, dull matte crust, no steam

keeping.note: A dense crumb and thick, dull crust hold up well for days, in keeping with the Alentejo tradition of baking large loaves to last.

method.title: Make a well
method.summary: Pile the flour in a large bowl or trough, hollow a well in the center, and add the starter.

method.title: Mix
method.summary: Gradually work in warm water and salt from the well outward until the flour is fully absorbed.

method.title: Knead vigorously
method.summary: Knead until air bubbles show in the dough, slapping it against the bowl to build strength.

method.title: Bulk ferment
method.summary: Cover and let rise undisturbed in a warm spot until well expanded.

method.title: Shape and proof
method.summary: Shape into round loaves, dust with flour, and let proof covered.

method.title: Bake without steam
method.summary: Bake hot, around 250°C dropping to 200°C, with no added steam, exactly what gives this bread its characteristic dull, thick crust.

source.adaptation: Starter assumed roughly 100% hydration to split into total-flour percentages; bulk and proof durations, left unspecified by the source, are typical estimates for this hydration and starter level.
